abstract: This paper investigates the problem of distributed consensus control for multi-agent systems characterized by general linear dynamics over directed communication topologies. Conventional consensus protocols often rely on the smallest non-zero eigenvalue of the graph Laplacian—a global parameter that limits the scalability and autonomy of the system. To overcome this limitation, we propose a distributed adaptive consensus protocol that requires only local agent dynamics and relative state measurements from neighbors. We demonstrate that this protocol ensures leader-follower consensus for any communication graph possessing a directed spanning tree rooted at the leader. Because the proposed control law is independent of global communication graph metrics, it is entirely distributed in nature. Furthermore, we extend our framework to accommodate more complex scenarios involving multiple leaders.
